taxonomy: Trochocephalus olivieri belongs to the phylum Platyhelminthes, specifically the class Rhabdocoela. This class is further divided into several orders, with Trochocephalus olivieri being part of the order Proseriata. The Proseriata order is characterized by flatworms that are typically small in size and have a simple body structure. Trochocephalus olivieri is a species of freshwater flatworm, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and habitat. As a member of the Rhabdocoela class, Trochocephalus olivieri shares similarities with other species in this group, but its specific traits set it apart.
